conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
You said walking the dog used to be enjoyable—what else has started to feel like a chore?
Cooking. I used to love trying new recipes, but now it just feels like too much effort. I find myself ordering takeout or just eating cereal because it’s easy.
When you think about the future, what comes to mind?
Nothing. I look at the calendar and I just don’t know what the point of it is.
You mentioned trying to focus on the day ahead—how easy is it for you to keep your mind on one thing?
It’s hard. I find myself staring at the wall, trying to remember what I was supposed to do, and then I realize I forgot.
Can you tell me more about how that has been affecting your day-to-day?
It’s making me miss things. I missed my friend’s birthday party because I couldn’t remember what day it was. It feels like I’m just going through the motions.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It feels like I’m watching someone else’s life. I see the photos on my phone, and I think, 'That's my life, but I'm not really there.'
When you feel like you're just watching your life go by, does that make you feel more on edge or restless?
Definitely. I wake up in the middle of the night and I just lay there, thinking, 'This is it. This is the rest of my life.'
You mentioned waking up in the middle of the night—what kind of thoughts keep you up?
The same ones. 'What am I doing with my life? Is this really all there is?' It's just this loop of uncertainty.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
I just feel lost. I look around at everyone else, and they all seem to have a purpose. I don't know what mine is anymore.
Is there anything else you'd like to share about how you've been feeling?
I just feel like I’m disappearing. I look in the mirror and I don't see the person I used to be.
When you say that, is it more about wanting to withdraw from everything, or feeling like you don't want to be here?
It’s both. I don't know how to be here anymore, and I don't know how to leave.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to get excited about stuff you normally enjoy during the day?
Yeah, it does. I used to love hiking, but now I just can't seem to muster the energy to go.
When you're lying awake thinking about what you could have done differently, what kind of things come to mind?
Just small stuff, like maybe I should have said something sooner or been more supportive. It's like I'm beating myself up over tiny things.
When you think about those small things you could have done differently, how does that make you feel about yourself?
It makes me feel really stupid. Like, I should have been more aware of what was going on, but I wasn't.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
It was after the breakup. I just couldn't stop thinking about what I did wrong, and it’s been months, but I still can’t shake it.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
Everything, I guess. Even things I used to enjoy, like going out with friends, just feel pointless right now.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I just feel stuck, like I'm waiting for something to change, but I don't know what that is or how to make it happen.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
It feels like both. I just feel so drained, like I can't keep this up anymore, but at the same time, I don't know what else to do.
Do you feel like people would be better off without you, or is it more that you feel stuck?
It's definitely more that I feel stuck. I don't want to be the person who brings everyone else down, but I just can't seem to stop thinking about the past.
